content.problemStatement:
A sophomore Computer Science student is frustrated with boring college lectures. Professor X agreed to give him some questions; if the student answers all questions correctly, then minimum attendance criteria will not apply to him.

Professor X chooses a sequence A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and asks QQQ queries. In each query, the student is given an integer PPP; he has to construct a sequence B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​, where P⊕Ai=BiP \oplus A_i = B_iP⊕Ai​=Bi​ for each valid iii (⊕\oplus⊕ denotes bitwise XOR), and then he has to find the number of elements of this sequence which have an even number of 111-s in the binary representation and the number of elements with an odd number of 111-s in the binary representation. Help him answer the queries.
constraints: **The input/output is quite large, please use fast reading and writing methods.**
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines contains a single integer PPP describing a query.
content.outputFormat: For each query, print a single line containing two space-separated integers ― the number of elements with an even number of 111-s and the number of elements with an odd number of 111-s in the binary representation.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The elements of the sequence BBB are P⊕4=7P \oplus 4 = 7P⊕4=7, P⊕2=1P \oplus 2 = 1P⊕2=1, P⊕15=12P \oplus 15 = 12P⊕15=12, P⊕9=10P \oplus 9 = 10P⊕9=10, P⊕8=11P \oplus 8 = 11P⊕8=11 and P⊕8=11P \oplus 8 = 11P⊕8=11. The elements which have an even number of 111-s in the binary representation are 121212 and 101010, while the elements with an odd number of 111-s are 777, 111, 111111 and 111111.
